message:
refactor(runtime): revert spawn dedup to inline and refine oneshot drop test

BlockingPool/BlockingPoolHandle: Revert the spawn_task_on_inner()
extraction back to inline implementations in each type. The TOCTOU
shutdown check in BlockingPool::spawn_with_priority no longer marks
the task as cancelled after enqueue — if the task was accepted before
shutdown, it should run during the drain phase. BlockingPoolHandle
retains the post-enqueue cancellation for safety since handle-spawned
tasks may not have the same drain guarantee.

BlockingOneshot: Refine the sender-drop test to use catch_unwind +
assert_with_log instead of #[should_panic], providing better
diagnostic output. Revert Arc::clone(&state) to state.clone() for
consistency. Separate the closed_without_result and result-consumed-
twice panic paths with an explicit if-else instead of the combined
assert+panic pattern. Reorder tests to keep spawn_blocking_propagates_
panic before blocking_oneshot_sender_drop_fails_closed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
